criteria.label: Apply the chain rule to differentiate nested elementary functions.
criteria.example: Compute d/dx of e^{x²} as 2x·e^{x²} and verify at x=1 against a central finite difference.

criteria.label: Decompose a composite function into inner and outer parts before differentiating.
criteria.example: For h(x)=sqrt(1+x²), set u=1+x² and show h′(x)=x/sqrt(1+x²) from f′(u)·u′.

criteria.label: Explain how one backward step in a tiny network follows the chain rule.
criteria.example: For z=wx, a=ReLU(z), L=(a-y)², write ∂L/∂w=(∂L/∂a)(∂a/∂z)(∂z/∂w) with numeric values at w=1, x=2, y=0.
